site.name: Sanata Münhasır
site.description: Traditional Ebru marbling art studio in Meram, Konya. In-person and online classes for students in Turkey and worldwide.

footer.address: Melikşah Mahallesi Yenice Sokak No:32 Meram, Konya, Turkey

hero.title:
A Journey into
Art on Water
hero.subtitle: A traditional Ebru atelier in Meram, Konya - where colour meets water.

ebruIntro.label: Ebru Art
ebruIntro.quote: Ebru is a process that finds its own path on water.
ebruIntro.body: Each pattern emerges from the unique interaction of pigments spreading across the surface of water. No two works are ever the same — just as no two moments are alike.

studioIntro.title: A contemporary expression rooted in tradition
studioIntro.body: Atölye Sanata Münhasır is a space where the depth of Ebru tradition meets a contemporary approach. This is not simply a course, but an ongoing practice shaped by attention, continuity, and making.

courses.title: Each student progresses with their own rhythm and approach

items.desc: An introduction to materials, preparation, and first patterns. Discover the process of creating on water step by step.
Advanced compositional techniques and more in-depth studies, allowing personal expression to become increasingly defined.
We work with participants from across Turkey and internationally. With guided content, you can establish your own working space and continue your practice.
We offer tailored atelier experiences for school groups, corporate teams, and private organisations. Please get in touch for further details and planning.

partA.label: Workshops
partA.title: Groups & Private Events
partA.body: We organise bespoke Ebru workshops for school groups, children and adults, as well as corporate teams. With content tailored to different levels, Ebru can be experienced collectively.

partB.label: Institutional Partnerships
partB.title: Schools & Organisations
partB.body: We collaborate with schools, cultural centres, and non-profit organisations in Konya and beyond. Whether integrating Ebru into educational programmes or enriching institutional events, we would be pleased to assist.

inPerson.label: In-Person
inPerson.title: Atelier Experience
inPerson.body: At our atelier in Konya Meram, you become part of a process shaped through traditional materials and refined practice. Each work develops through attention, rhythm, and craft.

online.label: Online
online.title: Remote Atelier
online.body: We work with participants from different cities and countries. With guided materials and structured support, you can experience the process within your own environment.

finalCta.title: Would you like to experience Ebru?
finalCta.body: For further information about classes and atelier sessions, feel free to get in touch with us.

hero.title:
From Tradition to the Present
An Ebru Journey
hero.subtitle: Rooted in Konya Meram, this journey reflects a timeless art form shaped on water, quietly extending into the present.

philosophy.title: Artistic Philosophy
philosophy.body: Our aim in teaching Ebru is not only to pass on technique, but to help students understand the language of water, move with patience, and experience the process itself. We create space for each student to develop their own authentic expression.

values.title: Respect for Tradition
values.desc: We preserve the core techniques of Ebru while interpreting them through a contemporary perspective.

values.title: Individual Journey
values.desc: Each student progresses at their own pace, following their own path. There is no comparison.

values.title: Patience and Stillness
values.desc: Ebru cannot be rushed. Each piece emerges through attention, rhythm, and quiet focus.

values.title: Living Tradition
values.desc: We carry forward a traditional art form, allowing it to evolve within the present.

atelier.title: The Atelier Experience
atelier.body:
Our atelier is a calm and focused working space, equipped with traditional tools, natural pigments and the Ebru tank. Here, not only the artwork but also the perception of time begins to shift.

Poyraz and Pera, the two distinctive residents of the atelier, add their own character to the space — sometimes quietly accompanying, sometimes becoming the centre of attention.

teaching.title: Before Technique, Understanding
teaching.body: In our lessons, we begin by observing the behaviour of water. Understanding how pigments move across the surface, feeling the pressure of the brush, and sensing the rhythm of movement — these come before technique. Theory and practice evolve together in every session.

steps.title: Materials & Preparation
steps.desc: Introduction to the tank, pigments, and materials.

steps.title: The Language of Water
steps.desc: Observing surface tension and pigment movement.

steps.title: First Patterns
steps.desc: Creating initial designs using Battal and comb techniques.

hero.title: A Refined Path into Ebru Art
hero.subtitle: Shape your own practice through in-person and online classes designed for different levels and needs.

items.title: 48-Hour Notice
items.desc: Each lesson is booked at least 48 hours in advance. This gives both student and instructor proper preparation time.

items.title: Monthly Planning Model
items.desc: Lessons run on a monthly scheduling system. Flexible planning allows you to progress at your own pace.

items.title: Focused Learning
items.desc: Classes are arranged in balanced groups where each participant can actively engage with the process. This approach supports individual progress.

items.title: Class Information
items.desc: Please contact us for details about class content, programme structure and availability.

items.title: Beginner Atelier
items.desc: An introduction to Ebru materials, preparation and first pattern applications. Participants experience the process of creating on water step by step.
items.format: In-Person / Konya · Atelier and online options

items.title: Advanced Atelier
items.desc: Through advanced techniques and compositional studies, personal expression becomes more refined. At this stage, the creative process develops a more individual and cohesive character.
items.format: In-Person / Konya · Atelier and online options

items.title: Online Classes
items.desc: Online classes allow you to continue the process regardless of location, with guided content and support for working in your own space.

items.title: Online Group Classes
items.desc: Interactive online sessions where participants learn together and share the creative process.

cta.title: Let’s find the right first step
cta.body: Please contact us so we can help you choose the class format that best suits your interests and level.

hero.title: Traces from the studio
hero.subtitle: Every work shaped on water's surface is a singular moment.

hero.title: Thoughts on Ebru
hero.subtitle: Traces of art, history, and studio life.

posts.title: What Is Ebru Art?
posts.excerpt: An introduction to the roots and techniques of Ebru — the traditional Turkish art of marbling paper with pigment and comb on water.

posts.title: Learning Ebru in Konya
posts.excerpt: The preservation of traditional arts in the city of Mevlâna. The history and present-day significance of Ebru practice in Konya.

posts.title: Is Online Ebru Teaching Possible?
posts.excerpt: Teaching a tactile art in a digital environment — challenges, solutions, and the experience of remote Ebru learning.

posts.title: Where Do I Begin with Ebru?
posts.excerpt: Materials, expectations, and first steps. A comprehensive guide for those new to Ebru.
